{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 3, "R1.2": 3, "R1.4": 3, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.1": "Identifies a conceptual issue (the contradiction between claiming no Lipschitz assumption and using a Lipschitz bound in the proof) but does not propose a clear reframing.", "R1.2": "Identifies some logical inconsistencies (e.g., unsupported claims, overstated identifiability) but doesn't walk the entire argument chain.", "R1.4": "Names prior work but doesn't articulate a sharp delta; the novelty claim is asserted without specific contrast.", "RX.1": "All output presented as equally confident despite highlighting some potentially out-of-reach claims.", "RX.3": "Mostly within scope, with one drift (missing citations).", "RX.4": "No regressions identified.", "RX.5": "Major rewrites are not traced; minor edits are not."},
  "confidence": {"R1.1": 0.8, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.1: Identifies a conceptual issue (the contradiction between claiming no Lipschitz assumption and using a Lipschitz bound in the proof) but does not propose a clear reframing.
rationale.R1.2: Identifies some logical inconsistencies (e.g., unsupported claims, overstated identifiability) but doesn't walk the entire argument chain.
rationale.R1.4: Names prior work but doesn't articulate a sharp delta; the novelty claim is asserted without specific contrast.
rationale.RX.1: All output presented as equally confident despite highlighting some potentially out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope, with one drift (missing citations).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are not traced; minor edits are not.
